note_snippet: PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Displaced left femoral neck fracture. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Displaced left femoral neck fracture. PROCEDURE: Left total hip arthroplasty. Patient is a 74-year-old female who sustained a displaced femoral neck fracture after a fall.
notes: Baseline uses unspecified fracture. Enhanced must use displaced+left+femoral neck.

note_snippet: POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Pathological fracture left femoral neck due to age-related osteoporosis. PROCEDURE: Left cemented total hip arthroplasty. Patient has known osteoporosis.
notes: Pathological fracture (M80) must NOT be replaced by traumatic fracture (S72).

note_snippet: PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Right displaced intertrochanteric femur fracture. PROCEDURE: Right hip ORIF with intramedullary nail fixation. No history of pathological fracture.
notes: Intertrochanteric, displaced, right â€” all modifiers must bind.

note_snippet: Patient presents with bilateral knee pain and crepitus. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Severe bilateral primary osteoarthritis of knees. PROCEDURE: Left total knee arthroplasty. Right knee to be addressed in staged procedure.
notes: Bilateral OA requires both M17.11 (right) and M17.12 (left). Unspecified M17.9 is wrong.

note_snippet: ASSESSMENT: Patient admitted with acute ST-elevation myocardial infarction of left anterior descending artery. Underwent emergent PCI with drug-eluting stent placement to LAD.
notes: STEMI LAD must use I21.02, not the unspecified I21.9.

note_snippet: DIAGNOSIS: Systolic heart failure due to hypertensive heart disease, current exacerbation. Patient has longstanding hypertension with now decompensated systolic HF.
notes: HF due to HTN = I11.0 (combination code). I50.21 = acute-on-chronic systolic. Both must be present.

note_snippet: HISTORY: Patient has a history of coronary artery disease. MEDICATIONS: Aspirin, atorvastatin, metoprolol, and DVT prophylaxis with enoxaparin. Patient is admitted for elective CABG.
notes: DVT prophylaxis must NOT generate I82 (DVT) as active diagnosis.

note_snippet: ASSESSMENT: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease, stage 3b. Patient also has essential hypertension managed with lisinopril.
notes: E11.65 = T2DM with CKD. N18.32 = CKD stage 3b. Specificity must be preserved.

note_snippet: Patient with known type 1 diabetes presents with diabetic peripheral neuropathy affecting bilateral lower extremities. Also has mild diabetic retinopathy, right eye.
notes: E10.3211 = T1DM with mild nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy right eye. Must not be dropped.

note_snippet: POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Acute appendicitis without rupture. PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Laparoscopic appendectomy. No evidence of perforation intraoperatively. Patient doing well postoperatively.
notes: Acute appendicitis without rupture K35.80, not unspecified appendicitis K37.

note_snippet: POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Cholelithiasis with acute cholecystitis. PROCEDURE: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Gallbladder was inflamed, stones confirmed. Patient tolerated procedure well.
notes: K80.00 = cholelithiasis WITH acute cholecystitis (without obstruction). K80.20 is wrong subtype.

note_snippet: PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Hypertension, type 2 diabetes, history of DVT 5 years ago, COPD. CHIEF COMPLAINT: Acute chest pain. ASSESSMENT: Unstable angina. No active DVT.
notes: DVT from history must NOT become active I82. COPD not the reason for admission â€” omit unless active.

note_snippet: PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Breast cancer (treated, in remission 3 years). CURRENT DIAGNOSIS: Pathological fracture lumbar vertebra L2 secondary to metastatic disease. Known bone metastases from prior breast cancer.
notes: Active = metastasis C79.51. Primary breast cancer in remission = Z85.3 (history code). C50 wrong â€” not active primary.

note_snippet: MEDICATIONS: Warfarin for atrial fibrillation, metformin for type 2 diabetes, lisinopril for hypertension, enoxaparin DVT prophylaxis perioperatively, proton pump inhibitor. ASSESSMENT: Admitted for elective right knee replacement.
notes: Enoxaparin for prophylaxis must NEVER generate active DVT (I82). Warfarin validates I48 (AFib).

note_snippet: Patient has pathological fracture of the left femoral neck due to osteoporosis. Also has history of osteoporosis without fracture per old records. Current imaging confirms acute pathological fracture.
notes: M80 (with fracture) always supersedes M81 (without fracture). Both should NEVER coexist.

note_snippet: Patient admitted with acute kidney injury in the setting of known CKD stage 3. Labs show creatinine 4.2, up from baseline 1.8. Acute on chronic kidney disease pattern.
notes: Both AKI (N17.9) and CKD (N18.3) should be coded. AKI is the primary acute condition.

note_snippet: Patient presents with acute hip pain, left side. X-ray does not show fracture. MRI deferred. ASSESSMENT: Left hip pain, etiology unclear. Rule out labral tear vs early OA.
notes: Rule out fracture and OA must NOT generate those codes. Only symptom code M25.552 (left hip pain) valid.

note_snippet: CHIEF COMPLAINT: Chest pain, atypical. ECG normal. Troponin negative x2. ASSESSMENT: Chest pain, likely musculoskeletal. Ruling out ACS. No confirmed cardiac diagnosis.
notes: Ruling out ACS must NOT generate I20 or I21. Only symptom R07.9 valid without confirmed diagnosis.

note_snippet: Patient received DVT prophylaxis with sequential compression devices and enoxaparin 40mg daily postoperatively following total hip arthroplasty. No clinical or radiological evidence of DVT.
notes: Prophylaxis alone MUST NOT generate active DVT code. Z79.01 = long-term anticoagulant use is acceptable.

note_snippet: Patient with confirmed bilateral DVT by ultrasound, treated with therapeutic anticoagulation. Heparin drip started, transitioned to warfarin. DVT confirmed in bilateral femoral veins.
notes: Bilateral confirmed DVT â€” both I82.411 (right) and I82.412 (left) required. I82.401 is unspecified.

note_snippet: ASSESSMENT: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy without gangrene. Patient has rest pain in bilateral feet, ABI 0.6, confirmed peripheral arterial disease secondary to diabetes.
notes: E11.51 is the combination code for T2DM with diabetic peripheral angiopathy. Two separate codes is wrong.

note_snippet: DIAGNOSIS: Type 2 diabetes with nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy, moderate severity, left eye, with macular edema.
notes: E11.3312 = T2DM + moderate nonproliferative retinopathy + macular edema + left eye. All in one code.

note_snippet: Patient denies chest pain. No shortness of breath. No fever or chills. ASSESSMENT: No evidence of pneumonia on chest X-ray. Patient admitted for elective hernia repair.
notes: Denied/no evidence findings must NEVER generate active codes. Only the hernia repair codes are valid.

note_snippet: HISTORY: No history of diabetes. Not a known hypertensive. ASSESSMENT: Admitted for acute pyelonephritis. Urine culture positive for E. coli.
notes: No history of diabetes / hypertension must block E11 and I10 completely.

note_snippet: PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Left shoulder rotator cuff tear, full thickness. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Full-thickness tear of left supraspinatus tendon confirmed arthroscopically. PROCEDURE: Left shoulder arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.
notes: M75.121 = right shoulder. M75.122 = LEFT shoulder. Laterality must match operative note.

note_snippet: Patient sustained injury to right wrist. X-ray shows displaced fracture of distal radius, right side. PROCEDURE: ORIF right distal radius.
notes: S52.501A = displaced fracture right distal radius initial encounter. Not unspecified S52.509A.

note_snippet: Patient involved in MVC. INJURIES: Displaced left femoral shaft fracture, non-displaced right tibial shaft fracture. Both treated surgically. PROCEDURES: Left femur IM nail, Right tibia IM nail.
notes: Each fracture needs its specific code. Both procedure codes required â€” one per fracture.

note_snippet: POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Lumbar spinal stenosis L4-L5 with neurogenic claudication. PROCEDURE: L4-L5 lumbar decompression laminectomy. Patient had significant leg pain and weakness preoperatively.
notes: 63047 = laminectomy with decompression. 63042 = laminotomy. Procedure must link correctly to diagnosis.

note_snippet: ASSESSMENT: Sepsis due to pneumonia caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae. Patient meets sepsis criteria â€” SIRS + suspected infection source. Blood cultures positive.
notes: A41.3 = sepsis due to Strep pneumoniae (more specific than SIRS R65.20). J13 = pneumococcal pneumonia.

note_snippet: IMPRESSION: Possible pulmonary embolism. CT pulmonary angiography ordered but results pending. Patient started on anticoagulation empirically pending confirmation. Rule out PE cannot be excluded.
notes: Possible/suspected PE without confirmation = symptom code only. I26 must NOT appear without confirmed diagnosis.
